etimes“Gen Z expenses” shock internet: 22-year-old Bengaluru woman’s spending habits go viralA Bengaluru woman's viral video detailing her "Gen Z expenses" sparked online debate. Her monthly spending includes flowers, therapy, brunch dates, massages, pedicures, and self-care items, totaling a significant amount. While some criticized her lifestyle as lacking responsibility, others lauded her focus on mental well-being and self-love, highlighting generational shifts in spending priorities.

etimesExplained: How these 5 animals sense danger before humansAnimals often sense danger long before humans, a survival instinct honed by superior senses. Dogs detect high-frequency sounds and vibrations, while elephants feel ground tremors. Birds react to air pressure changes, snakes sense vibrations, and cats possess acute hearing. These creatures' heightened awareness offers early warnings, a stark contrast to human reliance on visible cues.

etimesIIT graduate quits US bank job at 38, sells 4BHK, travels across 35 countries: “Titles didn’t feed my soul”An IIT graduate left a lucrative banking career to explore the world. He travelled for 18 months, visiting 35 countries. He met his wife, Kathy, in Montenegro. They now have three children and live a nomadic life, homeschooling them with a blend of ancient Indian texts and modern curriculum. Their lifestyle prioritizes experiences over material possessions.
